quoteFirst: Then the king, alarmed at the situation, dismissed Guizot and promised reform, and the people, satisfied for the time and proud of their victory, paraded the streets with cheers and songs. All now might have gone well but for a hasty and violent act on the part of the troops.
quoteLast: a shouting and torch-bearing throng marched through the Boulevards, singing and waving flags. Reaching the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, they halted and called for its illumination.
title: Famous Men and Great Events of the Nineteenth Century
author: Morris, Charles, 1833-1922

quoteFirst: The first of the hostile counter-attacks began
quoteLast: , when a wave of Turks who had crept along the landward slope and up to the crest in silence, burst over it with a yell and fell upon the British line. Fortunately, our men were not taken by surprise; a roar of musketry at close range received the enemy, and when it came to bayonet work our _morale_ proved more than sufficient to dispose of the foe.
title: The Tenth (Irish) Division in Gallipoli

quoteFirst: His chubby, smooth, innocent appearance was a reason for his being always treated with condescension when he was not put down. A stranger entering his own poor house at
quoteLast: might have been surprised to find him sitting up to supper. So boyish was he in his curves and proportions, that his old schoolmaster meeting him in Cheapside, might have been unable to withstand the temptation of caning him on the spot.
title: Our Mutual Friend
author: Dickens, Charles, 1812-1870

quoteLast: , a large herring-gull struck one of the south-eastern mullions of the Bell Rock Light House with such force, that two of the polished plates of glass, measuring about two feet square, and a quarter of an inch in thickness, were shivered to pieces and scattered over the floor in a thousand atoms, to the great alarm of the keeper on watch, and the other two inmates of the house, who rushed instantly to the light room.
title: The Mirror of Literature, Amusement, and Instruction. Volume 19, No. 539, March 24, 1832
